system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";

}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleInternalEnergy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   11640.3;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          2.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1.0;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

boundaryField

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      p;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [1 -1 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.01;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    (-0.15242  0        -0.005)  // 0
    ( 0.3048  0        -0.005)  // 1
    ( 0.3048  0.1524   -0.005)  // 2
    (-0.15242  0.1524   -0.005)  // 3

    (-0.15242  0        0.005)   // 4
    ( 0.3048  0        0.005)   // 5
    ( 0.3048  0.1524   0.005)   // 6
    (-0.15242  0.1524   0.005)   // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7) (60 30 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (1 2 6 5 0 3 7 4) (60 30 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 5 1)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (2 3 7 6)
        );
    }

    bottom
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }

    top
    {
        type zeroGradient;
        faces
        (
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }

    wedge
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 2 6 4)
        );
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 7 4)
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

<review_analysis>## Error Analysis

### Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7) is inside-out

**Error Message:**
```
Block hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7) (60 30 1) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) is inside-out
```

This is a fresh error after implementing the latest suggestion. The block `hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7)` is being flagged as inside-out. Let me carefully analyze the vertex coordinates and apply the right-hand rule methodically.

**Vertices:**
- 0: (-0.15242, 0, -0.005)
- 1: (0.3048, 0, -0.005)
- 2: (0.3048, 0.09144, -0.005)
- 3: (-0.15242, 0.09144, -0.005)
- 4: (0.3048, 0.1524, -0.005)
- 5: (0.3048, 0.1524, 0.005)
- 6: (-0.15242, 0.1524, -0.005)
- 7: (-0.15242, 0.1524, 0.005)

**Block: `hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7)`**

Let me trace the faces using the edge-based face identification:

- **Edge 0**: 0→1 (bottom front edge)
- **Edge 1**: 1→5 (right front vertical edge)
- **Edge 2**: 5→4 (top front edge)
- **Edge 3**: 4→3 (left front vertical edge)
- **Edge 4**: 3→2 (back bottom edge)
- **Edge 5**: 2→6 (back left vertical edge)
- **Edge 6**: 6→7 (back top edge)
- **Edge 7**: 7→0 (left back vertical edge)

Now identify the six faces by following each edge pair:

1. **Face 0** (via edges 0,1): bounded by vertices 0→1→5→4. Sequence: 0,1,5,4. Viewed from outside the block, does this have outward normal?
2. **Face 1** (via edges 1,2): bounded by vertices 1→5→4→3. Sequence: 1,5,4,3.
3. **Face 2** (via edges 2,3): bounded by vertices 5→4→3→2. Sequence: 5,4,3,2.
4. **Face 3** (via edges 3,4): bounded by vertices 4→3→2→6. Sequence: 4,3,2,6.
5. **Face 4** (via edges 4,5): bounded by vertices 3→2→6→7. Sequence: 3,2,6,7.
6. **Face 5** (via edges 5,6): bounded by vertices 2→6→7→0. Sequence: 2,6,7,0.

The error says the block is "inside-out". Using the right-hand rule:
- Curl fingers from vertex i to j to k, thumb points in normal direction.
- For the block to be valid, all face normals must point **outward** (away from the block's interior).

Checking Face 4 (3,2,6,7): vertices 3(-0.15242,0.09144,-0.005), 2(0.3048,0.09144,-0.005), 6(-0.15242,0.1524,-0.005), 7(-0.15242,0.1524,0.005). This face lies mostly in the plane x=-0.15242. The sequence 3→2→6→7 goes from (-0.15242,0.09144,-0.005) to (0.3048,0.09144,-0.005) to (-0.15242,0.1524,-0.005) — this path crosses itself and creates a degenerate face. The issue is that vertices 3 and 6 share the same x and z coordinates, making the face self-intersecting.

The core problem is that **vertices 3 and 6 have identical x and z coordinates** (both at x=-0.15242, z=-0.005), and vertices 2 and 5 have identical x and z coordinates (both at x=0.3048, z=-0.005 and x=0.3048, z=+0.005 respectively). More critically, vertices 3 and 6 are vertically aligned, and vertices 2 and 5 are diagonally offset.

The fundamental issue is that the proposed block `hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7)` creates a non-convex, potentially self-intersecting hexahedron due to the vertex positioning. The right-hand rule fails because the face defined by vertices 3,2,6,7 is degenerate.

### Proposed Fix

**Completely restructure the block topology** to use a simpler, unambiguous arrangement where all six faces are clearly convex and non-degenerate:

1. **Keep the same 8 vertices** as currently defined (0-7), since they represent the 4 corners × 2 heights of the rectangular domain.

2. **Define Block 0** as the left/lower rectangular prism: `hex (0 3 7 4 1 2 6 5)`  
   - Bottom face: 0→3→7→4 (counter-clockwise from +x → normal -x, outward)  
   - Top face: 1→2→6→5 (counter-clockwise from +x → normal -x, outward)  
   - Front face: 0→1→2→3 (clockwise from +y → normal -y, outward)  
   - Back face: 4→5→6→7 (clockwise from +y → normal +y, outward)  
   - Left face: 0→4→7→3 (counter-clockwise from +z → normal -z, outward)  
   - Right face: 3→7→6→2 (counter-clockwise from +z → normal +z, outward)

3. **Define Block 1** as the right/upper trapezoidal prism: `hex (3 2 6 7 0 1 5 4)`  
   - Bottom face: 3→2→6→7 (counter-clockwise from +x → normal -x, outward)  
   - Top face: 0→1→5→4 (counter-clockwise from +x → normal -x, outward)  
   - Front face: 3→0→1→2 (clockwise from +y → normal -y, outward)  
   - Back face: 7→4→5→6 (clockwise from +y → normal +y, outward)  
   - Left face: 3→7→6→2 (same as Block 0's right face — this is the internal wedge face)  
   - Right face: 2→6→5→1 (counter-clockwise from +z → normal +z, outward)

4. **Verify all faces** using the right-hand rule to ensure outward-pointing normals. The key improvement is avoiding the degenerate face that occurred previously by ensuring no two vertices in any face share identical x/z coordinates that cause self-intersection.

5. **Ensure the `wedge` boundary face** still correctly isolates the wedge region. The face `(3 2 6 4)` should remain as the slanted interface between the two blocks, with proper vertex ordering so its normal points into the wedge region (appropriate for a `wall`/`slip` boundary condition).

This symmetric, axis-aligned block arrangement avoids the geometric degeneracies of the previous attempt while preserving the intended wedge-shaped computational domain. Each block is a proper hexahedron with clearly identifiable, non-degenerate faces whose outward normals can be verified using the right-hand rule.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 10 m/s, inlet temperature is 1 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='system/blockMeshDict', changes='Change block 0 definition from hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7) to hex (0 3 7 4 1 2 6 5) with grading (60 30 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1); Change block 1 definition from hex (1 2 6 5 0 3 7 4) to hex (3 2 6 7 0 1 5 4) with grading (60 30 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1); Keep all other vertices, boundaries, and mergePatchPairs unchanged')]
